description: Alerting rules for the Security Finding Chain scenario (GuardDuty, Security Hub, Security Lake)

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Security Finding Chain — GuardDuty HIGH/CRITICAL Findings
rules.investigationGuide:
# GuardDuty HIGH/CRITICAL Findings

The threat detector raised a HIGH or CRITICAL finding.

## Triage
1. List findings: `FROM logs-aws.guardduty-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND aws.guardduty.severity.value IN ("High", "Critical") | KEEP @timestamp, aws.guardduty.severity.value, aws.guardduty.type, source.ip | SORT @timestamp DESC | LIMIT 25`
2. Identify the resource (instance ID or IAM user)
3. If Exfiltration → see Data Exfil runbook; if PrivEsc → see IAM runbook

## Escalate when
- **Always page security on-call** for HIGH/CRITICAL
- Customer data or secrets implicated

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Security Finding Chain — Multi-Stage Chain Burst
rules.investigationGuide:
# Multi-Stage Chain Burst

Multiple alert-class docs across detectors in a short window.

## Triage
1. Detector breakdown: `FROM logs-aws.guardduty-*, logs-aws.securityhub_findings-*, logs-aws.securitylake-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 30m AND event.kind == "alert" | STATS findings = COUNT(*) BY event.dataset | SORT findings DESC`
2. One detector spiking = less interesting; three = investigate
3. Check for shared source.ip or user.name

## Escalate when
- Spike crosses three or more detectors with shared pivot
- Includes HIGH/CRITICAL severity findings

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Security Finding Chain — Security Hub Compliance FAILED
rules.investigationGuide:
# Security Hub Compliance FAILED

Multiple controls marked FAILED — posture drift.

## Triage
1. Which controls: `FROM logs-aws.securityhub_findings-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND aws.securityhub_findings.Compliance.Status == "FAILED" | STATS failures = COUNT(*) BY aws.securityhub_findings.Compliance.SecurityControlId | SORT failures DESC | LIMIT 25`
2. One control repeating = single misconfiguration; many = broader drift
3. Check CloudTrail for recent resource creation

## Escalate when
- Failed control is in regulatory audit subset (PCI, HIPAA, SOC 2)
- Same resource fails multiple controls

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Security Finding Chain — Multiple GuardDuty Alerts With Source IP
rules.investigationGuide:
# Multiple GuardDuty Alerts With Source IP

Burst of findings with external source IPs — likely external scanner/attacker.

## Triage
1. Top IPs: `FROM logs-aws.guardduty-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.kind == "alert" AND source.ip IS NOT NULL | STATS findings = COUNT(*) BY source.ip | SORT findings DESC | LIMIT 10`
2. One IP = targeted; many IPs = broader campaign
3. Check threat intel feeds for the IP

## Escalate when
- Source IP on known threat feed
- Targets include customer-facing resources
